final_phrase: KEEP THE CITY REAL BEFORE THE FEED MAKES IT FLAT.
start_message:
THE MISSING REEL

My name is Nora Blake.

If you are reading this, my film has already disappeared from the digital world. My cloud folder is empty. My last post is gone. The Feed has swallowed my work, cut it into fragments, and tried to turn the city into background noise.

But I made a backup.

Not online.

I hid it in the real city.

Every place on your route keeps one frame. To restore the film, you must do what The Feed does not want you to do: stop, look carefully, read physical signs, notice space, and speak to each other.

The Feed wants you to scroll.
I need you to look.

Type START when your team is ready.
safety_message:
Before you begin, promise me three things.

First: stay in the real world. Do not run, do not cross roads dangerously, and do not enter private or closed spaces.

Second: stay together. The Feed separates people into private screens. This investigation works only if you speak to each other.

Third: use English. Not perfect English. Real English. Ask, suggest, disagree, correct yourself, try again.

If you agree, type I AGREE.
finish_message:
You restored the missing reel.

KEEP THE CITY REAL BEFORE THE FEED MAKES IT FLAT.

The Feed wanted the city to become content. You found depth, distance, weight, memory and space.

You did not just pass through the city. You read it as a real place.

Final voice task:
Record a 60-second team message in English.

Answer this question:
What part of today’s route felt most real to you, and what would The Feed probably flatten, crop or hide?

locations.title: Frame 1: The Source
locations.message:
FRAME 1: THE SOURCE

You are standing near a building that has changed its role.

The Feed would show you this place as a clean white background for a photo. It would flatten it into an image, add music, and move on after three seconds.

But I need you to look longer.

This place is cultural now. People come here for exhibitions, events, conversations and public life. But the building still carries an older memory. Its name and industrial shape point to something that made the modern city possible long before it became a place for art.

Look at the name of the building. Look at its form. Ask yourselves what this place once gave to the city before it became part of cultural life.
locations.question: What invisible force from this building’s former life made lights, machines, screens and transport systems work?
hints: Think about what a technical station usually produces, distributes or controls.
Without it, The Feed would go dark too.
locations.wrong_reply:
Too fast. That is how The Feed wins.

Look again at the building’s former function. The answer is not about art yet. It is about what powered the modern city.
locations.success_reply:
Correct.

Frame 1 restored: SOURCE.

The Feed depends on this force, but it wants you to forget the real infrastructure behind the screen.

Fragment 1 unlocked:

KEEP
locations.speaking_task:
Discuss in English:

What real-world systems does the digital world depend on? Name at least three.
locations.next_directions: Next frame: stay near GES-2. Move to a point where you can see the roofline and the vertical industrial elements of the building. Type ARRIVED when your team is ready.

locations.title: Frame 2: The Vertical Memory
locations.address: Area near GES-2, where the vertical elements are visible
locations.message:
FRAME 2: THE VERTICAL MEMORY

Stay near the building and look up.

The Feed loves smooth surfaces. It removes context, crops out history and turns architecture into a clean background. But buildings are not backgrounds. They remember how they were used.

I left this frame above eye level because people who only look at their phones often miss it.

The vertical elements of this building are not only decorative. They remind you that this place once belonged to industry, machines, heat, air, movement and technical systems.

Do not look for a screen.
Do not look at the pavement.
Look above the main façade.
locations.question: What visible vertical elements remind you of the building’s industrial past?
hints: Look above the main façade. These elements rise upward.
They look connected with industrial architecture, air, heat or technical systems.
locations.wrong_reply:
The Feed has cropped your view too low.

Raise your eyes and look for the parts of the building that point upward.
locations.success_reply:
Correct.

Frame 2 restored: MEMORY.

The Feed cuts off the top of the picture. You looked up and found what the image usually hides.

Fragment 2 unlocked:

THE
locations.speaking_task: Describe what people usually miss when they look at buildings only through a phone camera.
locations.next_directions: Next frame: move to the embankment / waterline near GES-2, where you can see the building and the water. Type ARRIVED when your team is ready.

locations.title: Frame 3: The Broken Copy
locations.message:
FRAME 3: THE BROKEN COPY

Move to a point where you can see the building and the water.

The Feed shows copies all the time: copied faces, copied poses, copied sounds, copied jokes, copied cities. A copy can be attractive. It can even look more dramatic than the original. But a copy is fragile.

Look at the water.

When the surface is calm, the city appears there again. When the surface moves, the image breaks. This is what happened to my film online: the city was repeated, compressed, distorted and finally lost.

The real building is still here.
The copy changes every second.

I need you to notice the difference.
locations.question: What do we call the image that appears when the city is repeated on a surface like water, glass or a mirror?
hints: You can see this effect in mirrors, windows and calm water.
The same word can also mean deep thinking.
locations.wrong_reply:
The Feed gives you copies and hopes you forget the original.

Look at the water again. Name the visual effect, not the building.
locations.success_reply:
Correct.

Frame 3 restored: COPY.

The Feed gives you copies. The real world gives you the source.

Fragment 3 unlocked:

CITY
locations.speaking_task:
Discuss:

Can an online image ever show the full reality of a place? Why or why not?
locations.next_directions: Next frame: go to the Patriarchal Bridge, the pedestrian bridge over the river. Type ARRIVED when your team is ready.

locations.title: Frame 4: The Crossing
locations.message:
FRAME 4: THE CROSSING

Go to the pedestrian crossing over the river.

The Feed teaches you to jump from one thing to another without noticing the space between them: one video, next video, next video, next video. No distance. No transition. No effort.

The real city works differently.

Here, you must physically cross from one side to another. You feel the wind, the height, the river, the distance, the view. This structure does not erase separation. It helps you move through it.

I left this frame here because the real world still understands transition.

The Feed skips.
The city connects.
locations.question: What structure allows people to cross the river without entering the water?
hints: You use it to move from one bank to another.
It connects two sides over an obstacle.
locations.wrong_reply:
That answer moves too quickly.

Think about the physical structure that makes this crossing possible.
locations.success_reply:
Correct.

Frame 4 restored: CONNECTION.

The Feed skips distance. You crossed it.

Fragment 4 unlocked:

REAL
locations.speaking_task: Explain the difference between “skipping” and “crossing” using this place as an example.
locations.next_directions: Next frame: move to a safe viewing point where you can see the large monument rising above the river. Type ARRIVED when your team is ready.

locations.title: Frame 5: The Giant
locations.location_name: View toward the Large Monument on the River
locations.address: Safe viewing point toward the Peter the Great monument
locations.message:
FRAME 5: THE GIANT

From a safe viewing point, look toward the huge monument rising above the river.

The Feed likes things that are big, dramatic and easy to react to. It asks: Do you love it? Do you hate it? Would you share it? Would you argue in the comments?

But the real city asks slower questions.

Why is this object here?
Why is it so large?
Why does it dominate the view?
What story does it want to tell?

Do not focus only on the human figure. Look at the object that carries the figure into a story of travel, water and exploration.
locations.question: What object connects the monument with movement across water?
hints: It is connected with navigation and long journeys.
It can move across seas and rivers.
locations.wrong_reply:
The Feed wants an instant reaction to the monument.

I need slower looking. Do not focus only on the person. Notice what carries the story.
locations.success_reply:
Correct.

Frame 5 restored: SCALE.

The Feed wants instant reaction. You gave careful attention.

Fragment 5 unlocked:

BEFORE
locations.speaking_task:
Discuss:

Why do large public monuments often create arguments? Use the phrase “because they…”
locations.next_directions: Next frame: continue toward Krymskaya Embankment until you can see different pedestrian routes or levels. Type ARRIVED when your team is ready.

locations.title: Frame 6: The Route Splits
locations.address: Krymskaya Embankment, select a point with visible pedestrian levels/routes
locations.message:
FRAME 6: THE ROUTE SPLITS

Move along the embankment until your team can see different ways pedestrians move through the space.

The Feed gives everyone the same gesture: thumb up, thumb down, swipe, scroll, repeat. It makes movement simple and almost automatic.

The real city gives you choices.

You can walk closer to the water or farther from it. You can move quickly or slowly. You can look at the river, the buildings, the people, the art, or your own reflection in a screen. The path you choose changes what you notice.

Stop for a moment and study the pedestrian space as a system, not just as a route.
locations.question: How many main pedestrian routes or levels can your team clearly identify from this point?
hints: Compare the route closer to the water with the route farther from or above it.
The answer is a small number.
locations.wrong_reply:
The Feed gives one endless path.

The city gives alternatives. Look again at how people can move through this space.
locations.success_reply:
Correct.

Frame 6 restored: CHOICE.

The Feed gives one endless path. The city gives alternatives.

Fragment 6 unlocked:

THE
locations.speaking_task:
Discuss:

How does your route change what you notice in a city? Give one example from this embankment.
locations.next_directions: Next frame: enter Muzeon Park of Arts and find the area with sculptures. Type ARRIVED when your team is ready.

locations.title: Frame 7: The Open-Air Museum
locations.message:
FRAME 7: THE OPEN-AIR MUSEUM

Enter the park area and slow down.

The Feed turns images into a stream. You see something, then immediately something else replaces it: a face, a joke, a dance, a disaster, an advertisement, a memory. Everything competes for the same tiny moment of attention.

This place asks for a different kind of looking.

The objects here do not disappear when you stop looking at them. They remain in space. You can walk around them. You can see their front, side and back. You can compare their size with your own body.

They do not move, but your position changes their meaning.

Look around carefully.
locations.question: What three-dimensional art form is collected throughout this park?
hints: These objects are not flat like paintings, photographs or phone images.
You can walk around them and see them from different sides.
locations.wrong_reply:
The Feed has made you think in flat images.

Look for objects with volume, weight and space around them.
locations.success_reply:
Correct.

Frame 7 restored: PRESENCE.

The Feed gives flat images. The real world gives bodies, weight and space.

Fragment 7 unlocked:

FEED
locations.speaking_task:
Choose one object nearby.

Describe it in three sentences:
1. What do you see?
2. How does it make you feel?
3. Why would a photo of it be incomplete?
locations.next_directions: Next frame: find a physical map, board or navigation sign in Muzeon. Type ARRIVED when your team is ready.

locations.title: Frame 8: The Offline Guide
locations.address: Physical map, board or navigation sign in Muzeon
locations.message:
FRAME 8: THE OFFLINE GUIDE

Somewhere in this area, find a board or sign that helps visitors understand where they are.

The Feed always wants to be your guide. It says: “Do not look around. Open the app. Follow the blue dot. Trust the search result. Let the screen decide what matters.”

But before the blue dot, people used physical orientation. They looked for boards, arrows, names, paths, landmarks and their own position in space.

I left this frame near a real-world guide because I want you to notice something important:

The city can help you navigate without asking for your data.
locations.question: What physical guide helps visitors understand the area without using a phone?
hints: It usually shows paths, places and the visitor’s position.
You often find it on a board near entrances, paths or important areas.
locations.wrong_reply:
The Feed wants you to open an app.

I need you to find the offline guide that helps people orient themselves in real space.
locations.success_reply:
Correct.

Frame 8 restored: ORIENTATION.

The Feed tracks you. The city can guide you without watching you.

Fragment 8 unlocked:

MAKES
locations.speaking_task:
Discuss:

What is the difference between using a phone map and using a physical map? Mention privacy, attention and memory.
locations.next_directions: Next frame: go to the New Tretyakov Gallery and stand where you can see the building name. Type ARRIVED when your team is ready.

locations.title: Frame 9: The Curated Memory
locations.message:
FRAME 9: THE CURATED MEMORY

Stand where you can see the name of the large cultural building.

The Feed gives you images without responsibility. It does not ask whether something should be remembered. It asks whether you will keep watching. It studies your reaction and gives you more of what holds your attention.

This building belongs to another system.

Here, images are selected, preserved, argued about, restored, displayed and studied. Not everything appears. Not everything disappears. Someone must decide what deserves public memory.

The Feed remembers what keeps you online.
This place remembers what should not disappear.

Look at the building name and identify the type of cultural place it is.
locations.question: What type of institution preserves and presents selected works of visual art to the public?
hints: It is not mainly a cinema, a theatre or a concert hall.
The English word begins with G.
locations.wrong_reply:
The Feed is a stream.

This place is not a stream. It selects, keeps and presents visual art. Look again at the type of institution.
locations.success_reply:
Correct.

Frame 9 restored: CURATED MEMORY.

The Feed remembers your reactions. A cultural institution remembers works, contexts and meanings.

Fragment 9 unlocked:

IT
locations.speaking_task:
Discuss:

What is the difference between an algorithmic feed and a curated collection? Use the words “attention” and “memory”.
locations.next_directions: Next frame: move to the final point near Gorky Park. Type ARRIVED when your team is ready.

locations.title: Frame 10: The Final Cut
locations.message:
FRAME 10: THE FINAL CUT

You have reached the final point.

When you started, The Feed still had an advantage. It had trained your attention to move quickly: search, guess, react, scroll, repeat.

But during this route, you did something different.

You found the real infrastructure behind the digital world.
You looked up and noticed industrial memory.
You saw how a copy breaks on the surface of water.
You crossed real distance instead of skipping it.
You studied a huge monument before reacting to it.
You noticed that a route can offer choices.
You walked around objects that cannot be reduced to a screen image.
You found an offline guide that did not track you.
You reached a place where images are preserved instead of endlessly replaced.

The Feed does not always destroy the city.
Sometimes it does something quieter.

It removes depth.
It removes weight.
It removes distance.
It removes context.
It turns a real place into a surface.

Now complete my final warning.
locations.question:
Complete my final message with one word:

KEEP THE CITY REAL BEFORE THE FEED MAKES IT ______.
hints: The missing word describes something without depth.
A screen has this quality. A real sculpture does not.
The opposite idea is three-dimensional.
locations.wrong_reply:
That answer is close, but my warning is about depth.

The Feed does not only make the city faster or louder. It can remove space, weight and volume.

Try one word that describes a world without depth.
locations.success_reply:
You restored the missing reel.

KEEP THE CITY REAL BEFORE THE FEED MAKES IT FLAT.

The Feed wanted the city to become content. You found depth, distance, weight, memory and space.

You did not just pass through the city. You read it as a real place.

Final voice task:
Record a 60-second team message in English.

Answer this question:
What part of today’s route felt most real to you, and what would The Feed probably flatten, crop or hide?
locations.speaking_task:
Final team task:
Record a 60-second voice message in English answering the final question.
